candidate_summary: Andean khipu-inspired LLM routing cascade: 4-tier primary-pendant-secondary-tertiary hierarchy with 3-knot-type request classification (atomic/multi-step/exceptional), decimal-position scaling factor 10^k for model-size selection, subsidiary correction loop with exception spawning, and multi-dimensional cord-attribute metadata (ply/length/end/color) encoding modality/budget/format/priority.
content_words: andean inca khipu quipu primary pendant cord
3-knot type figure-8 long single decimal hierarchy
subsidiary secondary tertiary quaternary correction exception
cord ply length end color spacing record-keeping
subsidiary correction loop primary-tier verify exception subsidiary spawn + cord-attribute metadata

results.name: Unified Routing Cascading LLMs (dekoninck2024)
results.rationale: Unified framework for routing+cascading LLMs. Shares generic 'cascade' and 'routing' lexicon but no khipu/knot/decimal/subsidiary/cord-attribute terminology. No 4-tier primary-pendant-secondary-tertiary hierarchy, no 3-knot-type request classification, no decimal 10^k model-size scaling, no subsidiary correction loop with exception spawning.

results.name: 3-Tier Routing Cascade Rule-Semantic-LLM
results.rationale: 3-tier rule/semantic/LLM cascade. Shares 'tier' and 'cascade' but uses 3-tier not 4-tier, no knot-type token classification, no decimal scaling factor, no subsidiary exception spawn, no cord-attribute metadata.

results.name: CascadeDebate Multi-Agent Cost-Aware Cascade
results.rationale: Multi-agent debate cascade with cost-awareness. No khipu hierarchy, no knot-type atomic/multi-step/exceptional classification, no decimal-position 10^k scaling, no subsidiary correction loop, no cord-attribute (ply/length/end/color) metadata.

results.name: Dynamic Model Routing Cascading Survey
results.rationale: Survey of dynamic model routing/cascading. Generic cascade taxonomy without 4-tier primary-pendant-secondary-tertiary structure, without 3-knot-type request taxonomy, without 10^k decimal scaling, without subsidiary exception spawning, without multi-dim cord metadata.

results.name: C3PO Optimized LLM Cascades Probabilistic Cost Constraints
results.rationale: Probabilistic cost-constrained cascade optimization. No khipu/quipu, no knot types, no decimal hierarchy, no subsidiary correction with exception, no cord-attribute metadata.

results.name: Self-Aware Token-Efficient Routing Cascading EMNLP 2025
results.rationale: Self-aware token-efficient routing+cascading. Shares 'routing' and 'cascading' generically but no 4-tier khipu hierarchy, no 3-knot-type classification, no decimal 10^k scaling, no subsidiary correction loop, no cord-attribute metadata.

results.name: Cascaded LM Cost-Effective Human-AI
results.rationale: Cascaded LM with human-AI cost-effectiveness. Human escalation conceptually adjacent to exception subsidiary but lacks 4-tier primary-pendant-secondary-tertiary structure, 3-knot-type classification, decimal 10^k scaling, and cord-attribute metadata.

results.name: Is Escalation Worth It Decision-Theoretic LLM Cascade
results.rationale: Decision-theoretic escalation in LLM cascades. 'Escalation' loosely analogous to τ_R escalation but no khipu/knot framing, no 4-tier hierarchy, no 3-knot-type classification, no decimal 10^k model-size scaling, no subsidiary correction loop, no cord-attribute multi-dim metadata.

decision_rationale: All 8 results are generic LLM routing/cascading papers sharing only single lexical token 'cascade' (and occasionally 'routing'/'tier'/'escalation'). None contain the candidate's distinctive khipu/quipu construct (4-tier hierarchy + 3-knot-type classification + decimal 10^k model-size scaling + subsidiary correction loop + cord-attribute multi-dim metadata). All below kw>=2/sem>=0.7/func>=0.7 thresholds. NOTE: primary scored 8 hits at sem 0.78-0.92 / func 0.78-0.92 on multi-tier cascade-routing adjacencies; verdict-level disagreement flagged (Pattern E aggregate-adjacency vs per-paper scoring).
